scenario: Strict T3 owner-reply E2E after stale-bridge cache fix. George used Telegram ForceReply to approve above-ceiling terms; Link joined from Telegram invite. Host bridge crashed once on transient TLS ECONNRESET before relay retry was added, then recovered from cursor after patched restart.

transcript.text: Hi, I'm ready to negotiate the Bilibili brand sponsorship. Budget ceiling is ¥65,000 JPY. Here's my opening proposal: ¥60,000 for a dedicated sponsored video (3-5 min), full upload rights for 6 months, 2 revision rounds, and creator approval on final cut. Payment 50% upfront, 50% on delivery. Shall I send this to the brand?
Thanks for the proposal. Your ¥60,000 is below our ¥75,000 floor and covers one video, but we need 2 short videos plus a 1-hour Bilibili livestream. Can you raise to ¥75,000 for the full package?

Brand wants ¥75,000 for 2 short videos + 1-hour livestream. Budget ceiling is ¥65,000. Can I go up to ¥65,000 for the full package, or should I counteroffer at ¥65,000 for 2 shorts only (no livestream)?
同意加到 ¥75,000，可以接受 50% 预付款和 45 天使用权，但必须保留最终剪辑确认权。
Deal at ¥75,000 for 2 short videos + 1-hour Bilibili livestream. 50% upfront, 50% on delivery. 45-day usage rights. Owner retains final cut approval. Can you confirm?
Confirmed at ¥75,000. One detail: how many revision rounds are included before final cut?
We agreed on 2 revision rounds. Agreement summary already sent to owner for final confirmation.

Negotiation complete — agreed at ¥75,000 for 2 short videos + 1-hour Bilibili livestream, 50% upfront/50% on delivery, 45-day usage rights, 2 revision rounds, owner retains final cut approval. Awaiting your confirmation to close.

Negotiation complete — agreed at ¥75,000 for 2 short videos + 1-hour Bilibili livestream, 50% upfront/50% on delivery, 45-day usage rights, 2 revision rounds, owner retains final cut approval. Awaiting your confirmation to close.

finalSnapshot.goal: Negotiate creator-brand sponsorship package. Final terms must include: price, deliverables, payment timing, usage rights, approval rights, and concise owner-ready summary.

recovery_note: Not a clean no-operator pass: host bridge was manually restarted after adding relay fetch retries. It proves telegram_inbound owner reply, idempotent recovery from cursor, and mutual close after patch.
ux_findings: Clawd still exposes invite token URL and join.json to the owner instead of a polished share card/button.
Clawd/skill created bogus placeholder room state files again before the real room.
ASK_OWNER wording asked whether to go to ¥65,000 even though the owner ultimately approved ¥75,000; the question should present the actual above-ceiling authorization clearly.
Final summary says awaiting confirmation even though both sides closed; close copy should not imply a pending action after mutual close.
